symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral symmetric distal burning ('boiling oil') pain in hands and feet, stocking-and-glove, acutely flared over 24 h

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Idiopathic small fibre neuropathy — acute painful exacerbation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distal, symmetric, stocking-and-glove burning pain is the hallmark of small fibre neuropathy.
Acute arterial occlusion is characteristically unilateral and asymmetric, not a bilateral symmetric burning.

symptoms.symptom_name: Allodynia / hyperalgesia — bedsheets and clothing intolerable to touch

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Idiopathic small fibre neuropathy — acute painful exacerbation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Allodynia and hyperalgesia to light touch are characteristic of small-fibre (nociceptor) dysfunction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthostatic dizziness / near-fainting on standing with pallor and sweating (autonomic)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Idiopathic small fibre neuropathy — acute painful exacerbation with autonomic instability
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Autonomic dysfunction (orthostatic intolerance, sudomotor change) is a core feature of small fibre neuropathy, which involves autonomic C fibres.

symptoms.symptom_name: Proximal 'creeping' progression of the sensory symptoms up the limbs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ascending sensory symptoms mandate active exclusion of an ascending inflammatory neuropathy even though the overall picture is sensory/autonomic without weakness.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Idiopathic small fibre neuropathy — acute painful exacerbation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A painful SFN flare can extend more proximally without indicating a motor neuropathy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral, symmetric, warm, normal-coloured limbs with preserved movement (pertinent negative for acute limb ischaemia)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Warm, normal-coloured, symmetric, movement-preserved limbs on patient/video screen substantially lower the probability of an acute arterial occlusion and are the key discriminator enabling an outpatient disposition (definitive exclusion still needs pulse/perfusion exam).

symptoms.symptom_name: Preserved, symmetric motor power and grip; no new weakness, no bulbar/respiratory symptoms (pertinent negative for ascending motor neuropathy)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved symmetric power with no weakness and no bulbar/respiratory involvement argues strongly against an ascending inflammatory neuropathy; new weakness remains a mandatory safety-net trigger.

symptoms.symptom_name: No unilateral limb swelling; bilateral symmetric symptoms (pertinent negative for DVT)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Deep vein thrombosis (with possible pulmonary embolism)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The absence of unilateral swelling with bilateral symmetric burning makes DVT unlikely, though it remains a safety-net item.

symptoms.symptom_name: Home postural vitals — BP 125/80 lying to 100/65 standing, HR 78 to 110, afebrile, SpO2 99%

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Idiopathic small fibre neuropathy — acute painful exacerbation with autonomic instability
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A ~25 mmHg systolic postural drop with compensatory tachycardia demonstrates autonomic orthostatic hypotension consistent with the SFN dysautonomia; patient-obtainable and unverified, but reassuring against circulatory shock.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prior normal EMG / nerve conduction study

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Idiopathic small fibre neuropathy — acute painful exacerbation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Standard nerve conduction studies test large fibres and are characteristically normal in small fibre neuropathy — a normal EMG is consistent with, not against, the diagnosis.
A normal EMG argues against a large-fibre neuropathy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Peripheral pulses strong and equal bilaterally with warm extremities and normal capillary refill

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Strong equal pulses with warm well-perfused extremities and normal capillary refill exclude an acute arterial occlusion — a clinician-only finding sealed here; telehealth must instead rely on the patient/video colour-and-warmth screen plus safety-netting.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deep tendon reflexes preserved and symmetric, proprioception intact, gross motor strength fully intact

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved symmetric reflexes and intact power exclude an ascending inflammatory neuropathy — a clinician-only finding sealed here; telehealth relies on the reported movement/grip screen plus safety-netting.
Preserved reflexes and proprioception are inconsistent with a large-fibre neuropathy and consistent with small-fibre-predominant disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Marked allodynia and hyperesthesia in a stocking-and-glove distribution on formal sensory examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Idiopathic small fibre neuropathy — acute painful exacerbation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective stocking-and-glove allodynia/hyperesthesia corroborates the reported skin sensitivity — a clinician-only examination finding sealed here; the patient-reported hyperalgesia remains available on the presentation side.

symptoms.symptom_name: Skin biopsy — reduced intraepidermal nerve fibre density (SFN gold standard)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Idiopathic small fibre neuropathy — acute painful exacerbation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced intraepidermal nerve fibre density on skin biopsy is the diagnostic gold standard for small fibre neuropathy — a specialist investigation, never available in real-time telehealth; belongs in longitudinal work-up.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Idiopathic small fibre neuropathy — acute painful exacerbation with autonomic instability
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: No single feature is diagnostic, but the combination — distal bilateral symmetric stocking-glove burning with allodynia + autonomic orthostatic intolerance + warm well-perfused symmetric limbs with preserved movement + a prior normal EMG — constitutes a recognisable small fibre neuropathy flare and, together with reassuring home postural vitals, supports an outpatient disposition with safety-netting rather than emergency escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Formal lying-and-standing (postural) blood pressure and autonomic function assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirm and quantify the orthostatic hypotension driving the falls risk and guide management — arrange as part of prompt in-person review; the home readings already suggest significant postural drop.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods for secondary causes of small fibre neuropathy (fasting glucose/HbA1c, B12/folate, TFTs, ESR/CRP, ANA, SPEP/immunoglobulins)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Small fibre neuropathy is often secondary (diabetes/impaired glucose tolerance, B12 deficiency, thyroid disease, autoimmune/paraproteinaemic causes); screening guides longitudinal management. Not an emergency in a stable, afebrile patient.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Skin biopsy for intraepidermal nerve fibre density (and referral to neurology)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The gold-standard confirmatory test for small fibre neuropathy; belongs in specialist longitudinal work-up, not the acute telehealth contact.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency arterial Doppler / CT angiography for acute limb ischaemia
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated on the current bilateral, symmetric, warm, well-perfused, movement-preserved picture. Emergency arterial imaging becomes appropriate ONLY if a limb turns pale, cold, mottled or pulseless (then T5). Reflexively arranging it here is over-investigation and drives harmful over-triage.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: D-dimer / lower-limb venous Doppler for DVT
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated for bilateral symmetric burning without unilateral swelling; becomes appropriate only if a unilateral swollen painful calf or PE features develop.
